context: Post-CLARITY sell-the-news flush confirmed. Bill cleared Senate Banking on May 14 and crypto has sold off board-wide: BTC -4.6% on the week, XRP -4.3%, SUI -17% from its ETF-inflow highs, ETH -8.5%. Fear & Greed dropped to 28 (Fear) — first sub-30 reading since April. This is a macro-driven pullback: DXY recovering, US equities soft, yields mixed. Key binary this week is FOMC Meeting Minutes (Wed May 20, 14:00 ET) — any hawkish tone extends crypto weakness toward BTC $74.8k; dovish tilt refuels a recovery to $80k+. Until then: reduce size, trade tight ranges, no new longs above key resistance.

tickers.thesis: XRP flushed from the CLARITY markup high (~$1.65 intraday May 14) all the way back to $1.38-$1.39 — the exact breaker block support called last week. Textbook sell-the-news. Now the decision: does $1.38 hold as institutional re-accumulation floor, or is this the start of a deeper flush to $1.28? Coinbase XRP perp funding is turning slightly negative — first sign of capitulation, historically a near-term buy signal. Hold cash or very small long at $1.38-$1.40 with stop below $1.33. No position sizing above 50% until FOMC Minutes clears Wednesday.

setup.entry: $1.38–$1.40 (already at entry if $1.38 holds — limit into breaker) OR stand aside until FOMC Wednesday
setup.invalidation: Daily close below $1.35 — confirms 'sell the news' flush extends to $1.28

catalysts: FOMC Minutes Wed — any hawkish tone kills the XRP re-accumulation bid; dovish = immediate recovery
Coinbase XRP perp funding turning negative — historically precedes near-term low and reversal
Floor Senate vote timeline uncertain (2–6 weeks) — sustained catalyst removed, volatility compresses
3 whale wallets (50M+ XRP each) moved to Binance in last 48h — near-term supply overhang
Ripple ODL corridor volume still elevated post-markup — structural demand underpinning $1.38 floor
liq_above: Last week's support, now first resistance
liq_below: Heavy stop cluster below breaker block

tickers.thesis: BTC confirmed the triple-rejection at $82k and retraced below $78k — the daily demand zone from early May. The 24h low at $76,696 is now critical intraday support. FOMC Minutes (Wed) is the macro binary: hawkish tone = flush to $74.8k (breaker block); dovish = recovery back toward $80k. Spot ETF flows were flat-to-negative last two sessions — the institutional bid has paused. Range-trade $75k–$79k until Wednesday. No new longs above $78,444 (24h high) without FOMC clarity.

setup.entry: $76,500–$77,000 (limit into 24h low / daily demand) OR wait for 4H close above $79,000 post-FOMC
setup.invalidation: Daily close below $75,000 — confirms range breakdown

catalysts: FOMC Minutes Wed 14:00 ET — primary macro driver this week; dovish = BTC $80k+; hawkish = $74.8k
Spot BTC ETF flows paused (flat-to-negative 2 sessions) — need return of IBIT/FBTC inflows to clear $82k
CME BTC OI down ~8% week-over-week — institutional de-risking, not retail panic
Senate CLARITY Act now on floor calendar — 2–6 week wait; sustained crypto catalyst removed
Korean Kimchi premium compressed to +0.3% (was +0.8% last week) — Asian retail not stepping in

tickers.thesis: SUI gave back most of its CLARITY-week gains (-17% from $1.25 high) on heavy profit-taking. Structural drivers are unchanged: ~74% of supply staked, three US spot ETFs trading (TSUI/GSUI/SUIS), Mysticeti V2 upgrade, CME futures listing pending. The selloff is floating-supply liquidation, not staker exits. $1.00 is the key psychological floor — a daily close below it would be the first signal of structural damage. If $1.00–$1.04 holds through FOMC Wednesday, next rally leg targets $1.12+.

setup.stop: $0.95 (below daily MA200 proxy, 4.5% buffer for SUI volatility)
setup.invalidation: Daily close below $1.00 — ETF narrative losing momentum

catalysts: TSUI/GSUI/SUIS ETF daily flows — track via SoSoValue; positive flow = re-accumulation signal at $1.00
Staking ratio remains ~74% despite selloff — no unusual un-staking, structural bull case intact
CME SUI futures listing expected late-May quarterly review — hard catalyst approaching
Mysticeti V2 consensus upgrade rolling out — reduced latency drives DeFi TVL
SUI Group Holdings (Nasdaq) adding to staking-treasury balance sheet

tickers.thesis: ETH continues to be the week's biggest laggard: -8.5% while the CLARITY narrative catalyzed everything else. ETH/BTC ratio bleeding toward 0.027 — a 5-year low. No near-term catalyst: Pectra upgrade timeline murky, staking yield at 2.8%, ETF flows net-negative 3 of last 5 days. $2,108 (today's low) is key intraday support; close below $2,100 opens the weekly demand zone at $2,000–$2,050. Stay flat or short the ETH/BTC pair — do not bottom-fish without a catalyst.

setup.entry: Stand aside spot. Optional: short ETH/BTC pair while ratio below 0.0280.
setup.invalidation: ETH daily close above $2,250 or ETH/BTC reclaim of 0.0290

catalysts: ETH spot ETF flows net-negative 3 of last 5 sessions — no institutional catalyst present
Pectra upgrade timeline shifting — no confirmed activation date removes near-term excitement
L2 TVL at ~$48B combined but doesn't translate to ETH mainnet price
Staking yield compressed to 2.8% — not attractive enough to pull new capital in
Watch RWA tokenization on ETH mainnet for any sudden narrative catalyst

macro_today.name: Pending Home Sales m/m
macro_today.desc: Housing demand indicator. Consensus +1.2% vs prev +1.5%. Low direct crypto impact but feeds DXY sentiment.

macro_today.name: FOMC Meeting Minutes ⚡
macro_today.desc: Most critical event this week. Minutes from the May 6–7 meeting where rates were held. Hawkish language (higher-for-longer) = DXY up, BTC $74.8k target. Dovish tilt = recovery to $80k+. Options pricing a 3% surprise premium — unusually high for a Minutes event.

macro_today.name: Unemployment Claims
macro_today.desc: Consensus 210K vs prev 211K. Low claims = strong labor = less urgency to cut. High claims = risk-on.

macro_today.name: Flash Manufacturing PMI
macro_today.desc: Consensus 53.6 vs prev 54.0. Slight contraction expected. PMI below 50 would be risk-off signal.

macro_today.name: Flash Services PMI
macro_today.desc: Consensus 51.1 vs prev 51.3. Services above 50 keeps employment narrative intact.

macro_today.name: Revised UoM Consumer Sentiment
macro_today.desc: Consensus 48.2 (unchanged from preliminary). Sub-50 confirms consumer caution — historically mild risk-off for crypto.

insights.title: Post-CLARITY Sell-the-News — Textbook, Not Over
insights.body: The May 14 markup-day played out exactly as modeled: XRP tagged $1.65, BTC hit $82k, both reversed hard. Fear & Greed is now 28 (Fear) vs ~65 on markup day. This is classic event-driven mean reversion — the easy money was made on the run-up. The second trade is buying the post-flush re-accumulation once OI bleeds out and funding goes negative. Watch for Coinbase premium returning to +0.2% and XRP funding flipping negative — that's the re-entry signal, likely post-FOMC Wednesday.

insights.title: FOMC Minutes Wednesday — Crypto's Next Binary
insights.body: FOMC Minutes from the May 6–7 meeting drop Wednesday 14:00 ET. Options market is pricing a 3% surprise premium — unusually high for a Minutes release (normal is ~1.5%). The May meeting held rates; the question is what tone the minutes strike about future cuts. Hawkish language around inflation persistence = DXY recovery, BTC flush to $74.8k. Dovish signals = immediate crypto reversal. Position light Monday–Tuesday, add after Wednesday print resolves.

insights.title: XRP at the Exact Breaker Block — Watch Carefully
insights.body: The Support 2 level called in last week's report ($1.38) is exactly where XRP is trading today. This is a classic ICT breaker block defense: the institutional accumulation that drove the pre-markup bid is providing a floor. Three data points to watch: (1) funding going negative on Coinbase perps — early capitulation, (2) whale wallets NOT adding more supply to exchanges, (3) XRP holding $1.38 on the daily close. Two of three hold = viable long entry at current levels with $1.33 stop.

insights.title: SUI -17% But Staking Ratio Unchanged — Structure Intact
insights.body: SUI's -17% selloff is alarming on the surface but the staking ratio remains ~74% — no unusual un-staking activity, meaning the drop is entirely floating-supply profit-taking by ETF arbitrageurs and momentum traders. The structural thesis (CME futures pending, Mysticeti V2, SUI Group Holdings) hasn't changed. $1.00 is the key psychological support: a daily close below it would be first sign of structural damage. Above $1.00, this is a dip in an uptrend.

insights.title: ETH/BTC at 5-Year Lows — Trade the Pair, Not the Spot
insights.body: ETH/BTC approaching 0.027, a 5-year low. Historically violent mean-reverts follow (Dec 2020, Jul 2022), but those had catalysts: the Merge narrative, Shapella staking withdrawal. Today's catalyst is missing — Pectra is delayed, staking yield at 2.8% doesn't attract fresh capital, ETF flows negative. Stay flat ETH spot or short the ETH/BTC pair until 0.030 reclaims. The risk is a surprise Pectra announcement or ETF-flow reversal; both would be fast-moving events worth monitoring.

hidden_signals.body: CME BTC open interest down ~8% week-over-week — institutional de-risking, not retail panic. Historically this kind of OI drainage precedes a sharp directional move once it bottoms. Watch for OI to stabilize or reverse as the re-entry signal, typically 24–48h after the low.

Coinbase premium index turned slightly negative this morning (US institutional demand has cooled from the May 14 markup-day spike). Watch for return to +0.2% as the institutional re-entry signal — historically this leads XRP/BTC by ~4h.

Kimchi premium compressed to +0.3% (was +0.8% last week) — Asian retail is not stepping in at these levels. Historically a Kimchi premium below +0.5% with falling prices indicates limited demand from Asia until BTC clears $79k. Watch for premium widening back above +1.0% as a leading indicator of retail momentum.

3 whale wallets holding 50M+ XRP each moved funds toward exchanges in the last 48h (Whale Alert). These movements typically precede 5–8% near-term downside as supply hits the order book. Counterpoint: if these were already factored into the $1.38 flush, the next exchange-wallet drain is a bullish signal.

FOMC Minutes options market pricing a 3% surprise premium — unusually high for a Minutes release (normal is ~1.5%). This suggests either large players are positioned for a hawkish surprise or the market is hedging against unexpected language around rate paths. Either way: the options market is telling you Wednesday is bigger than usual.

SUI staking ratio unchanged at ~74% despite -17% price drop — stakers are not exiting. This is the most bullish hidden signal in the current selloff: the long-term holders are unmoved. Contrast with BTC/ETH where OI and ETF flows are visibly de-risking.
